intro: × Laeliocattleya calimaniana, also known as Caliman Orchid, is a hybrid orchid species resulting from the cross between Laelia and Cattleya orchids. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in relieving respiratory issues, wound healing, and protecting against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of × Laeliocattleya calimaniana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use is primarily traditional and not extensively scientifically studied.
